preamble: Deaffrication. The lateral affricate [tɬ] simplifies to a simple alveolar stop [t], losing its lateral release. This often occurs during loanword adaptation in languages that lack lateral affricates, or as a dialectal simplification.

examples.note: Commonly seen in loanword adaptation or dialectal simplification in languages without lateral affricates.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
